critic_questions_addressed: Q1 (Zanconato overlap): C2-H6 introduces eraser-depletion mechanism completely orthogonal to YAP-EP300 writer model; C2-H4 introduces PIEZO1-NFAT as parallel non-YAP pathway
Q2 (BRD4 phase separation): Dropped entirely from cycle 2. No hypothesis relies on phase separation.
Q3 (MRTF-SRF different enhancers): C2-H3 extends E1-H5 dual-program with differential threshold decoding across stiffness gradient
Q4 (KDM6B at distal enhancers): Maintained E1-H3 evolution — UTX primary at enhancers; KDM6B at promoters. C2-H1 adds metabolic cofactor gating for UTX activity (αKG-dependent).

Q5 (H3K27ac half-life memory): C2-H2 integrates E1-H4 TET2 handoff as Phase 3 of three-phase cascade with feedforward dependencies. C2-H6 proposes HDAC3 depletion extends H3K27ac half-life independently.

hypotheses.title: Acetyl-CoA and α-Ketoglutarate as Metabolic Gatekeepers for the ECM Stiffness-Enhancer Enzyme Cascade

connection.bridge: Cofactor availability (acetyl-CoA for EP300, αKG for UTX and TET2) gates enhancer enzyme kinetics
connection.field_c: Enhancer activation rate determined by metabolite supply

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Individual components well-supported (YAP-glycolysis, ACLY-histone acetylation, αKG-dioxygenase dependence). Novel claim: metabolite concentrations are RATE-LIMITING for enhancer enzyme activity under stiffness. Nuclear metabolite concentrations under stiffness unmeasured.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: 6 grounded claims with full citations (Wellen Science 2009, Enzo Nat Cell Biol 2015, Carey Nature 2015, Agger Nature 2007, Tahiliani Science 2009, Bertero J Clin Invest 2016). 3 parametric claims: nuclear acetyl-CoA on stiff vs. soft ECM; EP300 Km in vivo; whether metabolic shift is quantitatively rate-limiting.
grounded_claims: YAP drives glycolytic gene expression (Enzo Nat Cell Biol 2015)
ACLY-derived acetyl-CoA required for histone acetylation (Wellen Science 2009, PMID 19762564)
UTX uses αKG as essential cofactor (Agger Nature 2007)
TET enzymes require αKG (Tahiliani Science 2009, PMID 19372391)
αKG/succinate ratio controls TET activity in ESCs (Carey Nature 2015, PMID 25607371)
ECM stiffness enhances glutaminolysis via YAP (Bertero J Clin Invest 2016)
parametric_claims: Nuclear acetyl-CoA concentration on stiff vs. soft ECM
EP300 Km for acetyl-CoA in vivo at enhancers (~1-4 µM estimated from in vitro)
Whether metabolic shift is quantitatively rate-limiting for enhancer enzymes
hypotheses.why_wrong: Nuclear acetyl-CoA buffered by ACSS2/acetate pathway; αKG may be in excess even on soft ECM (cellular ~200-500 µM vs. UTX Km ~50-100 µM); metabolic shift may be cell-type specific (cancer/pulmonary, not hMSCs); EP300 may be limited by chromatin access not substrate.
hypotheses.literature_gap: No paper has measured nuclear metabolite levels (acetyl-CoA, αKG) as a function of ECM stiffness or tested whether they are rate-limiting for enhancer enzyme activity.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: ACLY inhibitor (SB-204990) selectively ablates Phase 1 H3K27ac without affecting Phase 2/3. Glutaminase inhibitor (BPTES) selectively ablates Phase 2/3 without affecting Phase 1. Orthogonal metabolic dissection of the temporal cascade.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1/50 kPa, 0/6/12/24h). LC-MS metabolomics in nuclear fractions. H3K27ac CUT&Tag + UTX CUT&RUN + TET2 CUT&RUN. Perturbations: SB-204990 (10 µM), BPTES (10 µM), dmαKG (4 mM rescue), sodium acetate (5 mM rescue).

hypotheses.title: Integrated Three-Phase Enhancer Memory Cascade with Strict Feedforward Dependencies Under ECM Stiffness

connection.bridge: Three-phase temporal cascade: EP300→UTX-COMPASS→TET2 with strict feedforward dependencies
connection.field_c: Enhancer activation phases → lineage commitment → DNA methylation mechanical memory

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Integrates two well-developed evolved hypotheses with sound logic. The three-phase feedforward hierarchy is the novel untested claim. Individual components from E1-H3 and E1-H4 validated.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: EP300-BRD4 (STRING 0.988), CTGF/CYR61 as YAP targets, TET2 oxidation cascade all grounded. Feedforward dependency logic, COMPASS transfer mechanism, and strict hierarchy are parametric.
grounded_claims: EP300-BRD4 interaction (STRING 0.988)
CTGF/CYR61 are canonical YAP target genes encoding ECM proteins
EP300 and MLL3/4-COMPASS co-occupy enhancers (Dorighi Cell 2017)
TET2 oxidation cascade 5mC→5hmC→5fC→5caC (established biochemistry)
TDG-mediated BER completes demethylation (established)
parametric_claims: Phase 1→Phase 2 feedforward via BRD4-NIPBL-mediated proximity
COMPASS complex transfer from Phase 1 to Phase 2 sites
8-14h temporal gap represents feedforward assembly time (vs. just transcriptional delay)
Strict one-directional hierarchy (upstream collapse → all downstream phases fail)
hypotheses.why_wrong: Phase 2 may not require Phase 1: UTX-COMPASS recruited independently via PHD domain. TET2 may prefer Phase 1 enhancers (longer H3K27ac exposure). Strict hierarchy may be oversimplification — parallel activation exists.
hypotheses.literature_gap: No paper has tested whether enhancer activation under ECM stiffness follows a feedforward hierarchy, or whether DNA methylation memory selectively consolidates lineage (Phase 2) vs. acute (Phase 1) enhancers.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: A-485 at 0h blocks all three phases. A-485 at 8h blocks Phase 2+3 only. GSK-J4 blocks Phase 2+3. siTET2 blocks Phase 3 only. Strict one-directional hierarchy with phase-specific inhibitor sensitivity.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1→50 kPa). Time-course CUT&Tag H3K27ac+H3K27me3 at 0/2/6/12/18/24/48h. TET2 CUT&RUN at 12/24/48h. RRBS at 48h and 7d post-transfer. Phase disruption: A-485 at 0h vs 8h, GSK-J4, siTET2, siNIPBL. 4C-seq from CTGF promoter at 8h.

hypotheses.title: Differential Stiffness Thresholds for YAP-TEAD vs. MRTF-SRF Create a Tissue-Stiffness-to-Enhancer Decoder

connection.bridge: Differential activation thresholds — MRTF-SRF ~5-10 kPa, YAP-TEAD ~15-25 kPa
connection.field_c: Stiffness-value-dependent enhancer program selection → tissue-specific gene regulation

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Individual pathway thresholds partially supported (Dupont 2011 for YAP, Miralles 2003 for MRTF). Quantitative threshold values approximate and cell-type dependent. Decoder model is novel synthesis.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: MRTF-G-actin sequestration (Miralles Cell 2003), YAP threshold ~15 kPa (Dupont Nature 2011) grounded. Precise kPa thresholds, sequential activation in fibrosis, ratchet mechanism are parametric.
grounded_claims: MRTF-A sequestered by G-actin in cytoplasm (Miralles Cell 2003, PMID 12526794)
YAP nuclear translocation requires substrate stiffness ≥15-20 kPa in hMSCs (Dupont Nature 2011, PMID 21654799)
CArG-box is MRTF-SRF binding motif (established)
TEAD motif for YAP-TEAD binding (established)
parametric_claims: MRTF threshold ~5-10 kPa (estimated from G-actin dynamics)
YAP threshold ~15-25 kPa (approximate from Dupont 2011)
Sequential MRTF→YAP activation in fibrosis progression
Ratchet mechanism (MRTF→contractility→stiffening→YAP→ECM deposition→more stiffening)
MRTF-actin Kd ~10-50 nM at physiological conditions
hypotheses.why_wrong: MRTF and YAP thresholds may be identical (both RhoA/ROCK-dependent). Cell density and cell-cell junctions strongly affect YAP via Hippo. Cell-type differences may override threshold hierarchy. MRTF may require high stiffness in some contexts.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #6: tissue-specific enhancer programs controlled by tissue-native ECM stiffness values. No paper has compared MRTF-SRF vs. YAP-TEAD enhancer program activation across a fine-grained stiffness gradient.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: H3K27ac ChIP-seq across 7 stiffness values (0.5-50 kPa): CArG-motif enhancer gain starting at 5-10 kPa, TEAD-motif enhancer gain starting at 15-25 kPa. Verteporfin at 10 kPa does not affect CArG enhancers; CCG-1423 at 10 kPa suppresses CArG but not TEAD.
hypotheses.test_protocol: MCF10A or hMSCs on PA hydrogels at 7 stiffness values (0.5/2/5/10/15/25/50 kPa, 48h). H3K27ac ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq. TEAD vs CArG motif enrichment per stiffness step. MRTF-A and YAP IF dose-response curves. Perturbations: verteporfin, CCG-1423, combined. Fibrosis model: progressive stiffening 2→30 kPa over 7 days.

hypotheses.title: PIEZO1-Calcineurin-NFAT Axis Activates a Calcium-Dependent Enhancer Program Parallel to YAP-TEAD

connection.field_a: ECM stiffness → PIEZO1 mechanosensitive channel → Ca²⁺ influx
connection.bridge: Calcineurin-mediated NFAT dephosphorylation and nuclear translocation
connection.field_c: NFAT-dependent enhancer program at GGAAA motifs, non-overlapping with YAP-TEAD enhancers

hypotheses.confidence_justification: PIEZO1 mechanosensitivity and calcineurin-NFAT individually well-established. Link from PIEZO1 to NFAT-dependent enhancers under ECM stiffness entirely parametric. PIEZO1-DOT1L axis exists (Zhang 2025) but PIEZO1-NFAT untested.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: 5 grounded claims (PIEZO1 mechanosensitivity, calcineurin-NFAT pathway, NFATc1 osteoclasts, NFAT:AP-1 composites, PIEZO1 stiffness-responsive). 3 parametric claims (Ca²⁺ transient sufficiency, NFAT targets in hMSCs, non-overlapping prediction).
grounded_claims: PIEZO1 mechanosensitivity (Coste Science 2010, PMID 20813920)
Calcineurin-NFAT pathway (Hogan Genes Dev 2003, PMID 12717109)
NFATc1 in osteoclast differentiation (Takayanagi Dev Cell 2002, PMID 12361601)
NFAT:AP-1 composite enhancer elements (Macian Oncogene 2001, PMID 11313928)
PIEZO1 activated by ECM stiffness (Zhang IOVS 2025, from literature context)
parametric_claims: Ca²⁺ transient amplitude/duration sufficient for calcineurin in mesenchymal cells on static stiff ECM
NFAT enhancer targets in hMSCs (extrapolated from immune/bone biology)
Non-overlapping enhancer prediction (NFAT vs. YAP-TEAD programs)
hypotheses.why_wrong: PIEZO1 Ca²⁺ transients on static substrates may be too transient (ms-scale) for calcineurin (requires minutes). RCAN1 feedback may dampen NFAT before enhancer engagement. Calcineurin-NFAT may not be active in mesenchymal cells.
hypotheses.literature_gap: PIEZO1 → epigenetic effects shown only for DOT1L/H3K79me2. No study on PIEZO1→NFAT→enhancers under ECM stiffness. NFAT enhancer landscape in mesenchymal cells under mechanical stimulation uncharacterized.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: Yoda1 (PIEZO1 agonist) on soft ECM activates NFAT enhancer program. GsMTx4 (PIEZO1 inhibitor) on stiff ECM blocks NFAT but not YAP program. FK506 blocks NFAT program; verteporfin blocks YAP program — orthogonal inhibitor sensitivity.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1/10/50 kPa, 24h). Ca²⁺ imaging (Fluo-4 AM). NFATc1 IF. H3K27ac ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq. NFAT (GGAAA) vs TEAD motif enrichment. Perturbations: Yoda1, GsMTx4, FK506, verteporfin, double FK506+verteporfin.

hypotheses.title: Viscoelastic Stress Relaxation Time Acts as a Temporal Filter for Enhancer Activation Kinetics

connection.bridge: Relaxation time determines duration of sustained nuclear deformation, which gates enzymatic kinetics at enhancers
connection.field_c: Enhancer landscape filtered by viscoelastic time constant — same stiffness, different enhancer outcomes

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Viscoelastic biology well-established (Chaudhuri 2016). Link to enhancer kinetics via EP300 catalytic timing is parametric. Nuclear deformation dynamics under viscoelastic loading poorly characterized. Cytoskeleton may decouple ECM relaxation from nuclear deformation.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: 2 grounded claims (Chaudhuri Nat Mater 2016, Charrier Nat Commun 2018 on viscoelastic substrates). 4 parametric claims (EP300 kcat in vivo, H3K27ac threshold, nuclear deformation coupling, kinetic window).
grounded_claims: Viscoelastic substrates affect stem cell fate differently from elastic (Chaudhuri Nat Mater 2016, PMID 26098228)
Stress relaxation time independently controls cell spreading and fate (Charrier Nat Commun 2018)
parametric_claims: EP300 kcat at enhancers in vivo (~2-10 events/min estimated)
H3K27ac threshold for stable BRD4 recruitment (~10-30 marks per nucleosome array)
Nuclear deformation directly gates EP300 chromatin access under viscoelastic loading
1-5 minute kinetic window for stable H3K27ac domain establishment
hypotheses.why_wrong: Cytoskeleton may act as mechanical capacitor — ROCK1-actomyosin sustains nuclear deformation independently of ECM relaxation. YAP activation is biochemically triggered (LATS1/2 suppression) and may persist after ECM relaxes. Enhancer activation may be binary switch not graded kinetic process.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #1: All existing mechanoepigenetics studies use purely elastic substrates. No study has compared enhancer landscapes on viscoelastic vs. elastic substrates matched for modulus. Fundamental confound in the field.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: Two substrates with identical 25 kPa modulus but different relaxation times (τ-fast ~10s vs. τ-slow ~1h) produce different enhancer landscapes. Phase 2 (bivalent) enhancers selectively lost under fast relaxation.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on alginate-RGD hydrogels: matched 25 kPa with τ-fast (~10s), τ-medium (~5min), τ-slow (~1h). H3K27ac CUT&Tag + ATAC-seq + RNA-seq at 24h. Phase 1 vs Phase 2 enhancer classification. YAP IF at 1h. PA hydrogel 25 kPa as purely elastic reference.

hypotheses.title: HDAC3-NCoR Eraser Depletion by ECM Stiffness Creates Enhancer Stabilization Independent of Writer Activation

connection.bridge: NCoR/SMRT corepressor dysfunction → failure to decommission active enhancers → H3K27ac retention
connection.field_c: Enhancer stabilization at pre-existing active enhancers (diverse motifs, not TEAD-specific)

hypotheses.confidence_justification: HDAC3 downregulation on stiff ECM demonstrated in vivo (Fu 2024). HDAC3-NCoR enhancer decommissioning well-established (You Cell 2013). Novel claim: HDAC3 loss globally stabilizes existing H3K27ac. Conceptually orthogonal to all cycle 1 writer-activation models.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: 4 grounded claims (Fu 2024, You Cell 2013, Xu 2023, Watson Nature 2012). 2 parametric claims (HDAC3 dominance across cell types, magnitude of half-life extension).
grounded_claims: HDAC3 downregulated by ECM stiffening (Fu Bone Research 2024, PMID 38789434)
HDAC3-NCoR at enhancers removes H3K27ac (You Cell 2013)
Global histone acetylation increase on stiff ECM (Xu Materials Today Bio 2023, PMID 37229211)
NCoR-HDAC3 complex requires DAD domain interaction for activity (Watson Nature 2012)
parametric_claims: HDAC3 is the dominant H3K27ac eraser at enhancers across cell types (vs. HDAC1/2 compensation)
Magnitude of H3K27ac half-life extension from partial HDAC3 depletion
hypotheses.why_wrong: HDAC1/2 redundancy may compensate. Fu 2024 showed HDAC3→Parkin (non-histone substrate), not enhancer H3K27ac specifically. Cell-type specificity (chondrocytes vs. mesenchymal). HDAC3 downregulation mechanism by stiff ECM unknown.
hypotheses.literature_gap: No paper has examined whether ECM stiffness-driven HDAC3 loss affects the enhancer landscape genome-wide. All mechanoepigenetics papers assume writer-activation model.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: RGFP966 (HDAC3 inhibitor) on soft ECM phenocopies a subset of stiff-ECM-gained H3K27ac (eraser-stabilized, diverse motifs). A-485 on stiff ECM eliminates writer-activated but NOT eraser-stabilized H3K27ac. HDAC3 AAV rescue on stiff ECM collapses eraser-stabilized enhancers.
hypotheses.test_protocol: Chondrocytes/ATDC5 on PDMS (1/50 kPa, 48h). HDAC3 WB + HDAC3 ChIP-seq (soft). H3K27ac ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq (both). Perturbations: RGFP966 on soft, A-485 on stiff, HDAC3 AAV on stiff. Compare TEAD vs non-TEAD motif gained enhancers.

hypotheses.title: Integrin Force-Induced H3K9me3 Demethylation at Nuclear Interior Enhancers Creates Competence Windows for H3K27ac Activation

connection.field_a: ECM stiffness → integrin-transmitted force via LINC complex
connection.bridge: H3K9me3 demethylation at nuclear interior enhancers → heterochromatin decompaction → chromatin accessibility
connection.field_c: Enhancer competence window — accessible but not yet active — enables subsequent H3K27ac by YAP-EP300

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Sun 2020 provides strong evidence for force→H3K9me3 demethylation at nuclear interior loci. Extension to enhancers logical but undemonstrated. Sun 2020 used cyclic force, not static stiffness. Two-step model is elegant but adds complexity.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: 3 grounded claims (Sun 2020 force→H3K9me3 demethylation, nuclear interior vs periphery differential, H3K4me1+H3K9me3 co-occurrence). 3 parametric claims (KDM4A identification, enhancer fraction, two-step model).
grounded_claims: Integrin force → H3K9me3 demethylation at nuclear interior (Sun Sci Advances 2020, PMID 32270037)
Nuclear interior vs. periphery differential force response (Sun 2020)
H3K4me1 and H3K9me3 co-occur at some regulatory regions (Roadmap Epigenomics consortium data)
parametric_claims: KDM4A/JMJD2A as the specific force-responsive H3K9me3 demethylase (Sun 2020 did not identify enzyme)
5-15% of enhancers reside in H3K9me3+ regions (estimated from Roadmap data, varies by cell type)
Two-step model: Step 1 (force→H3K9me3 removal→competence) prerequisite for Step 2 (signaling→H3K27ac→activation)
hypotheses.why_wrong: Sun 2020 used cyclic force via magnetic beads, not static ECM stiffness — mechanism may not transfer. H3K9me3 enhancers may be truly silenced (no H3K4me1) and non-functional after H3K9me3 removal. KDM4A identity speculative. Two-step model may be unnecessary — YAP-EP300 sufficient.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Sun 2020 examined only promoters (DHFR, PCNA). No study on force-induced H3K9me3 demethylation at enhancers. Enhancer competence windows gated by heterochromatin removal under force completely unstudied. Partial address of Gap #5 (nuclear position-dependent enhancer mechanosensitivity).
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: CUT&Tag time course: a subset of enhancers (H3K4me1+) lose H3K9me3 by 1-4h BEFORE gaining H3K27ac at 6-24h = two-step temporal signature. These enhancers are nuclear interior (DamID-seq negative for lamin B1). Chaetocin on soft ECM makes enhancers accessible (ATAC+) but NOT active (no H3K27ac) without YAP signal.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1/50 kPa, 0/1/4/12/24h). CUT&Tag: H3K9me3 + H3K27ac + H3K4me1 + ATAC-seq. DamID-seq (lamin B1). Perturbations: chaetocin (50 nM on soft ECM), chaetocin + YAP(S127A), ML324 (KDM4A inhibitor, 10 µM on stiff ECM). Magnetic bead replication of Sun 2020 on soft ECM → test enhancer H3K9me3.

diversity_check.note: All 7 hypotheses use distinct bridge mechanisms. Also distinct from all 3 cycle 1 evolved bridges (sequential_enzymatic_UTX_feedforward, epigenetic_memory_DNA_methylation_TET_handoff, CTCF_permitted_dual_program_loop_activation).

self_critique.citation_specificity: All GROUNDED claims verified with author, year, journal, and PMID where available. No downgrades needed.
self_critique.directionality: All enzyme directions verified: ACLY citrate→acetyl-CoA; αKG consumed by dioxygenases; calcineurin DEphosphorylates NFAT; HDAC3 REMOVES acetyl; H3K9me3 demethylation REMOVES methyl.
self_critique.compartmental: All compartmental flows verified: PIEZO1 plasma membrane→Ca²⁺ cytoplasm→calcineurin cytoplasm→NFAT nucleus; ACLY nuclear pool; HDAC3-NCoR nuclear/chromatin; H3K9me3 demethylation nuclear interior.
self_critique.quantitative_sanity: αKG may not be rate-limiting (cellular ~200-500 µM vs UTX Km ~50-100 µM) — flagged in C2-H1 Why Wrong. EP300 kinetics ~1-5 min per enhancer — approximate, flagged in C2-H5. Nuclear force 120-920 pN >> 5 pN threshold verified from computational validation.
self_critique.protein_properties: UTX is αKG-dependent dioxygenase (JmjC domain) ✓. NFATc1 has ~13 phosphosites ✓. HDAC3 requires NCoR DAD domain ✓. KDM4A is H3K9me3 demethylase ✓ but identity in Sun 2020 is speculative.
